metric: Tehran sugar consumption (low estimate)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Ministerie van Waterstaat Handel en Nijverheid (Netherlands), p.27
notes: Based on 10.6 tons/day. Article notes this was 'believed to be probably higher'; an alternative contemporaneous estimate assuming 200,000 population and 60g/person/day gives 4,300 tons/year (4,500 incl. surrounding villages).

metric: Tehran sugar consumption (higher contemporaneous estimate)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing same Dutch legation source
notes: Includes villages around Tehran who purchased in the city. Two-thirds of this was loaf sugar (qand).

metric: Sugar consumption per capita
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Nationaal Archief (Netherlands), Legatie Perzie, 15 July 1898
notes: Article explicitly flags this and the 1890 estimates as 'clearly too high, given the fact that the population of Tehran only reached the 200,000 level by 1920' -- included for completeness but transcribed with the source's own skepticism preserved.

metric: Mazandaran sugar exported to Russia
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Govt of Great Britain DCR Annual Series no.4398 (1910), p.30
notes: 2,165 puds (1 pud = 16.38 kg). By this point Mazandaran cane-sugar exports to Russia had already 'diminished over time' from earlier in the 19th century.

metric: Mazandaran sugar exported to Russia
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing same source
notes: 948 puds. Sharp single-year drop from the 1891 figure, illustrating the terminal decline of the old Mazandaran cane-sugar export trade.

notes: Iranian/fiscal year 1906-07. VISUALLY VERIFIED via downloaded table image sugar_table_1.jpg (Read tool, no OCR). Russia dominated loaf-sugar imports throughout this period.

metric: Sugar share of total Persian imports
notes: Article states sugar was 'the most important import commodity' after cottons that year.

metric: Russian market share of Persian loafsugar imports (value)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Entner, table 13, p.72
notes: Fiscal year 1907-08. Loafsugar was 85% of all sugar imports; Russia held an 80% share of that. ~100 qerans = 1 French Franc per the article's own conversion note (as of 1912).

metric: Sugar refining state monopoly factories
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Floor 1984, p.30
notes: Kahrizak factory (originally opened 1895/96, closed 1899/1900) resumed operation in 1932, followed by seven more state-owned mills. Employed 4,200 workers total; output 34,000 metric tons/year -- see next two rows.

metric: Sugar refining workforce (state monopoly factories)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Floor 1984, p.30
notes: Same 8-factory state monopoly system as the row above.

metric: Sugar output (state monopoly factories)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Floor 1984, p.30
notes: Same 8-factory state monopoly system as the two rows above.

metric: Annual sugar consumption
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Roberts 1948 ('Iran. Economic and Commercial Conditions'), pp.18,21
notes: 67% of this was imported; Iran had just come off relying on the International Emergency Food Council (through 1948) and was newly exposed to world-market vagaries.

metric: Sugar-mill milling season (low end of range)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Agah 1958, pp.230-31
notes: Iranian year 1936-37. Illustrates poor capacity utilization/cost-competitiveness of the state mills; contrast with the 1956-57 figures in the next two rows.

metric: Sugar-mill milling season (high end of range)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Agah 1958, pp.230-31
notes: Iranian year 1936-37, companion to the row above.

metric: Sugar-mill milling season (low end of range)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Agah 1958, pp.230-31
notes: Iranian year printed in the source article as '1656-57', an unambiguous typographical error for '1956-57' (given the sentence directly parallels '1936-37' two decades earlier, and no sugar mills of any kind existed in Iran in the 1650s per this same article's own history section). Year corrected to 1956 here; value transcribed exactly as printed.

metric: Sugar-mill milling season (high end of range)
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Agah 1958, pp.230-31
notes: Same typo-corrected year as the row above; longer/more efficient milling season than 1936-37, consistent with the narrative of gradual efficiency improvement.

metric: Haft Tepe state sugar-cane mill capacity
source: Encyclopaedia Iranica, SUGAR, citing Aresvik 1976, pp.75-76
notes: Khuzestan; beet sugar still represented 90% of total Iranian sugar production even after this cane-sugar mill came online.

metric: Annual sugar consumption
notes: Up from 650,000 tons in 1970; per-capita consumption reached almost 30 kg/year by this point, driven by population and income growth.

notes: Mainly in Kermanshah and Fars provinces. Compare to FAOSTAT's own Iran sugar-beet production series (data/processed/agriculture_qcl_production.csv), which independently covers this same year -- included here for completeness of the narrative source, not because FAOSTAT lacks it.
